cases.question: How many paid annual leave days do employees receive per year?
cases.answer: Employees receive 10 paid annual leave days per year (Source 1).

cases.question: How many remote work days per week are allowed?
cases.answer: Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week, with manager approval (Source 1).

cases.question: What is the meal reimbursement limit?
cases.answer: The meal reimbursement limit is $50 per day (Source 1).

cases.question: Compare the annual leave policy in these documents.
cases.answer:
Summary:
- 2024 policy: 10 paid annual leave days; no carryover. (Source 1)
- 2025 policy: 15 paid annual leave days; up to 5 days may be carried over. (Source 2)

Per document:
- benefits-2024.pdf: Employees receive 10 paid annual leave days per year; unused leave cannot be carried over into the next year. (Source 1)
- benefits-2025.pdf: Employees receive 15 paid annual leave days per year; up to 5 unused leave days may be carried over into the next year. (Source 2)

Agreements:
- Both define paid annual leave for employees and specify a number of days per year. (Sources 1, 2)
- Both include an explicit carryover policy (whether allowed or not). (Sources 1, 2)

Differences:
- Leave entitlement: 10 days (2024) vs 15 days (2025). (Sources 1, 2)
- Carryover: none allowed (2024) vs up to 5 days allowed (2025). (Sources 1, 2)

Gaps or uncertainty:
- No details on accrual method, eligibility differences (e.g., part-time, tenure), approval process, blackout periods, or payout on termination. (No evidence in Sources 1–2)
- Effective dates or whether 2025 supersedes 2024 are not stated. (No evidence in Sources 1–2)
- Both documents provide direct annual-leave evidence; none lack relevant evidence.

cases.question: What is different about the remote work policy in these documents?
cases.answer:
Summary:
2025 increases the allowed remote work from 2 to 3 days per week while keeping the manager approval requirement (Source 1; Source 2).

Per document:
- benefits-2024.pdf: Employees may work remotely 2 days per week with manager approval (Source 1).
- benefits-2025.pdf: Employees may work remotely 3 days per week with manager approval (Source 2).

Agreements:
- Remote work is permitted with manager approval (Source 1; Source 2).

Differences:
- Number of allowed remote days per week: 2 days (2024) vs. 3 days (2025) (Source 1; Source 2).

Gaps or uncertainty:
- No details provided on eligibility exceptions, scheduling rules, or fully remote options; only the number of days and approval requirement are specified in the excerpts (Source 1; Source 2).

cases.question: Compare the meal reimbursement limit in these documents.
cases.answer:
Summary:
- 2024 policy: 50 dollars per day meal reimbursement limit (Source 1).
- 2025 policy: 60 dollars per day meal reimbursement limit (Source 2).
- Net change: +10 dollars per day from 2024 to 2025 (Sources 1–2).

Per document:
- benefits-2024.pdf: “The meal reimbursement limit is 50 dollars per day.” (Source 1)
- benefits-2025.pdf: “The meal reimbursement limit is 60 dollars per day.” (Source 2)

Agreements:
- Both documents set a per-day meal reimbursement limit (Sources 1–2).

Differences:
- Amount: 50 dollars/day in 2024 vs 60 dollars/day in 2025 (Source 1 vs Source 2).

Gaps or uncertainty:
- Currency is stated as “dollars” but not explicitly specified as USD (Sources 1–2).
- No additional conditions provided (e.g., taxes/tips inclusion, location-specific rules, or eligibility details) in the cited excerpts (Sources 1–2).

cases.question: What is the parental leave policy?
cases.answer:
The retrieved evidence does not include a parental leave policy.  
It only covers annual leave (10 paid days; no carryover) and remote work (2 days/week with manager approval) (Source 1; Source 2).  
Please provide the relevant document or location for the parental leave policy so I can confirm the details.

cases.question: Compare the hotel reimbursement policy in these documents.
cases.answer:
Summary:
- Only travel-guide.pdf states a hotel reimbursement policy: capped at 180 dollars per night and requires manager approval (Source 2).
- benefits-2025.pdf does not state any hotel reimbursement terms; it only mentions a meal limit of 60 dollars per day (Source 1).

Per document:
- benefits-2025.pdf: No hotel reimbursement policy found. Mentions meal reimbursement limit of 60 dollars per day (Source 1).
- travel-guide.pdf: Hotel reimbursement capped at 180 dollars per night; manager approval is required (Source 2).

Agreements:
- None on hotel reimbursement. Only one document specifies hotel terms.

Differences:
- Scope: travel-guide.pdf covers hotel reimbursement with a cap and approval requirement (Source 2); benefits-2025.pdf is silent on hotel reimbursement and instead specifies a meal limit (Source 1).

Gaps or uncertainty:
- Whether benefits-2025.pdf contains any hotel policy elsewhere or intends to defer to the travel guide.
- Whether the 180 dollars per night cap includes taxes/fees or varies by location (not specified in Source 2).
- Whether manager approval is required for all hotel reimbursements or only in specific cases (Source 2 does not clarify).
